because.text: First mechanized proof in the project, opening the formal-verification front accepted from the Qwen web review. Layer 1 (checked algebra): SizeBound.lean models the seven priced actions of Book I s3.4 as exact accounting effects and proves by induction that every reachable (size, spent) satisfies size <= spent + 1, plus the preflight corollary size <= budget + 1 (the bound v0.5.2 clarified must never be a live fault trigger). Lean 4 core only, no mathlib, checks in under a second, toolchain pinned. Layer 2 (checked premise): proofs/bridge_check.py drives the reference oracle step-by-step over adversarial terms (duplication towers, Omega, deep REF chains, dead branches, TV fixtures) and asserts every observed action satisfies the step-level premise the proof consumes (delta-size <= cost - 1): 861 steps, 0 violations. Layer 3 (pinned ends): the existing vectors and property P7. NOT yet mechanized and stated so in proofs/README.md: byte-level machine correspondence and Book II interfere invariants - the next targets. Both layers wired into CI (.github/workflows/proofs.yml, elan-pinned). Hand-written antecedents credited: Gemini gate proof, DeepSeek re-derivation (Book I s3.4).

subject.note: Executable law: the s3.4 memory bound is a machine-checked theorem (Lean 4, proofs/SizeBound.lean) with the step premise bridge-checked on live oracle traces
